message:
feat(fp-io): SqlConnection::truncate_table for fast table reset (br-phum / fd90.23)

Add truncate_table(table, schema) -> Result<(), IoError> trait method
for clear-without-drop semantics. Default impl emits DELETE FROM
<table> (universal SQL, slower on large tables but works on every
backend). PostgreSQL/MySQL impls should override with TRUNCATE
TABLE for the DDL-fast-path reset that also clears auto-increment
sequences. Schema arg routes through quote_identifier with the
standard supports_schemas() gating.

Free-fn truncate_sql_table(conn, table, schema) wraps the trait
call; re-exported via the frankenpandas umbrella.

5 new tests cover: SQLite default DELETE clears rows preserving
schema, schema-Some silently-ignored on SQLite, invalid table-name
rejection, multi-schema backend records schema-qualified DELETE,
backend override emits TRUNCATE TABLE keyword.

307 fp-io tests pass; clippy -D warnings clean across fp-io +
umbrella; ubs Critical=0.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -72,6 +72,7 @@ pub use fp_io::{
     list_sql_schemas,
     list_sql_tables,
     sql_table_schema,
+    truncate_sql_table,
     read_csv,
     read_csv_str,
     read_csv_with_options,
